BaseSkeletonNode (XbView)

BaseSkeletonNode is a helper class for the Scene class. It encapsulates the Skeleton class, which is a resource helper class of XBR. This allows the scene to abstract itself from the XBR class and its helper classes and provides a simpler interface for drawing a scene from the XBR resources themselves.

There are two constructors for this class:

  • The first takes a pointer to the Skeleton class it will abstract and a pointer to the XBR class that is passed down to the list of BaseFrameNode classes encapsulated by this class. This constructor iterates through the list of frame nodes and creates a BaseFrameNode for each frame node inside the skeleton. It then iterates through the new list of BaseFrameNode classes and constructs the skeleton hierarchy by setting the BaseFrameNode children and siblings to the correct values. The Scene class and helper classes do not actually use these children and siblings pointers at present, but the code is included so that classes that inherit from these base classes have access to them should they need it.
  • The second constructor takes an Effect structure pointer and is used when you have a model that is not attached to any skeleton. This constructor creates a BaseSkeletonNode for the model so that it can fit into the scene hierarchy. It then creates a BaseFrameNode from the model passed in and adds that single node to the list of frame nodes stored in the BaseSkeletonNode.

Although BaseSkeletonNode has many methods, only a few are described in detail:

  • Draw
  • Sort
  • Update
